data.title: Judge rules against health insurers in price-fixing case filed by healthcare providers
data.description:
• A federal judge in Chicago ruled that health insurers, including Cigna (NYSE:CI), UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH), and CVS Health (NYSE:CVS) subsidiary Aetna must face lawsuits accusing them of conspiring to underpay claims to healthcare providers.
  • The cases center around MultiPlan, now known as Claritev, which handles out-of-network claims for the insurers. MultiPlan, along with the insurers, are named as defendants.
  • In a statement provided to Reuters, attorneys for the plaintiffs wrote the defendants "orchestrated a cartel through the sharing of competitively sensitive confidential pricing information to illicitly coordinate on out-of-network reimbursements."
  • In his ruling, U.S. District Judge Matthew Kennelly wrote that "whether or not MultiPlan's calculated rates are labeled as 'recommendations,' the plaintiffs plausibly allege that they are more akin to mandates."
  • Aetna told Reuters that "we stand ready to argue the substantive facts of the case and defend ourselves vigorously in this matter."

data.title: Cactus raised at Barclays on 'transformative deal' for Baker Hughes surface pressure control unit
data.description:
Cactus (NYSE:WHD) +1.1% in Wednesday's trading as Barclays upgraded shares to Overweight from Equal Weight with a $54 price target, raised from $51, following this week's acquisition of a 65% stake in Baker Hughes' surface pressure control business for $345M.

Cactus (NYSE:WHD) made a "transformative deal," and the upgrade reflects confidence in Cactus' (WHD) strategic acquisition and its potential to drive growth and expand its international presence, Barclays analyst David Anderson wrote, adding that a more balanced portfolio and "considerable upside" to margins solidifies the company as the "go-to quality smidcap name" in energy services.

The SPC business generates all its revenue internationally, with 85% coming from the Middle East, the largest oil and gas market, and Cactus' (WHD) strategic move should help the company scale up in this region, which is anticipated to grow through the end of the decade, Anderson said.

The acquisition also offers Cactus (WHD) entry into a less cyclical, project-based market, and provides earnings visibility supported by long-term contracts, with 30% of SPC's revenue coming from aftermarket services, the analyst noted.

data.title: J&J sends AnaptysBio lower with mid-stage trial data for anti-inflammatory agent
data.description:
Despite premarket gains, shares of AnaptysBio (NASDAQ:ANAB) turned lower on Wednesday following disappointing mid-stage trial data for Johnson & Johnson’s (NYSE:JNJ) anti-inflammatory agent JNJ-67484703, designed similar to ANAB’s lead asset rosnilimab.

An abstract released ahead of a European medical event on rheumatology indicated on Wednesday that JNJ-67484703, which targets the T cell receptor PD-1, appeared safe and well-tolerated in a Phase 2 trial.

While the open-label study involved patients with autoimmune disorders, rheumatoid arthritis, ulcerative colitis, and Sjogren’s disease, the investigators had to discontinue the trial’s UC arm due to slow recruitment and high withdrawal rate.

San Diego, California-based AnaptysBio (NASDAQ:ANAB) announced encouraging long-term data from a Phase 2 trial for its PD-1-targeting anti-inflammatory agent rosnilimab on Tuesday, sending its shares higher in the post-market trading.

Concurrently, the company reaffirmed plans to release initial data from a Phase 2 trial for rosnilimab in ulcerative colitis in Q4 2025.

data.title: AMD seeks to boost AI software prowess with Brium acquisition
data.description:
• AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) announced on Wednesday that it has acquired open source software company Brium in an effort to further its artificial intelligence prowess.
  • “This acquisition strengthens our foundation for long-term innovation,” AMD said on its website. “It reflects our strategic commitment to AI, particularly to the developers who are building the future of intelligent applications. It is also the latest in a series of targeted investments, following the acquisitions of Silo AI, Nod.ai, and Mipsology, that together advance our ability to support the open-source software ecosystem and deliver optimized performance on AMD hardware.”
  • Terms of the deal were not disclosed.
  • AMD shares rose 1.4% in late afternoon trading.

data.title: International Paper is top pick in paper packaging stocks: UBS
data.description:
UBS on Wednesday initiated coverage on four U.S. paper-packaging producers, assigning a lone Buy rating to International Paper (NYSE:IP), and Neutral ratings on Packaging Corporation of America (NYSE:PKG), Graphic Packaging Holding (NYSE:GPK) and Sonoco Products (NYSE:SON). The report, authored by analyst Aojja Shah, outlines a cautiously optimistic medium-term outlook, with the industry expected to rebound in 2026 following a sluggish 2025.

“The industry is at a crossroads,” Shah wrote, noting that while valuation across the sector appears “fair,” key macroeconomic pressures are offsetting tailwinds such as announced capacity closures. The firm sees more compelling upside for 2026 as demand improves and recently announced closures support pricing and operating rates.

Containerboard: Weak 2025, brighter 2026

UBS forecasts a 1.0% decline in 2025 box shipments based on “The Corrugator,” its in-house statistical model, which integrates macroeconomic data to predict volume growth with 80% historical accuracy. Despite muted demand, producers have announced net closures totaling 2 million tons of capacity, roughly 5% of the market. That should help operating rates hold steady at around 90% this year, with a projected increase to 93% in 2026, levels last seen in 2021.

IP (NYSE:IP) stands out among the group. UBS highlighted Chief Executive Andrew Silvernail’s aggressive turnaround strategy, including a resource optimization plan and the $650 million synergy opportunity from the DS Smith acquisition. IP (IP) is targeting $5.5 billion to $6.0 billion in earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization by 2027, up from an estimated $3 billion in 2024 on a pro forma basis. UBS believes the market is undervaluing these efforts.

“Using the long-term average multiple of 8.5 times, we estimate that the market is embedding 2027 ebitda of only $4.7 billion, about 20% below company guidance,” the report said. “We like the plan.”

Packaging Corp (NYSE:PKG), though considered a strong operator, received a Neutral rating due to what UBS views as a balanced risk/reward profile in the current environment.

Boxboard: Volume woes and competitive headwinds

The boxboard segment – Graphic Packaging (NYSE:GPK) and Sonoco (NYSE:SON) -- faces greater headwinds, with operating rates falling below 90% across all substrates and demand stagnating. While capacity closures may provide temporary relief, new entrants are expected to keep pressure on volumes and margins.

Graphic Packaging (GPK), which has seen recent volume declines, plans to shift toward share buybacks following a heavy capital expenditure cycle, something UBS believes could offer “downside protection,” but not enough to warrant a more bullish rating.

For Sonoco (SON), UBS noted that the company has transformed its portfolio with the $4 billion acquisition of Eviosys, making it the world’s biggest producer of metal food and aerosol cans. However, with leverage near 4 times and limited clarity on synergy realization or debt reduction, the stock remains a hold for now. Sonoco (SON) is targeting leverage of 3.0 to 3.3 times by the end of 2026.

IP leads, industry recovery hinges on 2026

UBS's initiation reflects a broader industry in flux. While near-term caution dominates the outlook for boxboard and containerboard alike, IP’s (IP) self-help initiatives and synergy-driven ebitda growth earn it a standout Buy rating.

“Valuation on most stocks seeks fair,” Shah said, “We believe IP's ability to achieve its cost savings targets should be independent of market conditions.”

data.title: Constellation Energy slips as Citi downgrades after Meta nuclear deal
data.description:
Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) -3% in Wednesday's trading as Citi downgraded shares to Neutral from Buy, citing limited near-term upside following the stock’s recent rally and news of its power purchase agreement with Meta.

The 20-year PPA beginning in 2027 is priced in the $70-$95/MWh range, Citi’s Ryan Levine estimated, which adds ~$12/share of value compared to a no-deal scenario but does not offer a substantial premium for low-carbon nuclear power, the analyst said.

"This deal has broad implications on the power markets as it signals future contracting activity," Levine wrote, but he believes much of the benefit from such agreements is priced into Constellation's (NASDAQ:CEG) the stock price, which has surged since Citi's upgrade in April.

Levine said ~70% of Constellation's (CEG) existing nuclear plants with license expiring before 2045 could secure similar datacenter deals at ~$80/MWh, but he sees a “balanced risk to the upside and the downside” for the stock, maintaining a High Risk classification due to volatility in power prices, policy uncertainty, and execution challenges related to plant restarts.

data.title: Retail investors are shifting away from the Mag 7 and towards promising beat-up names – Robinhood
data.description:
Retail investors are beginning to rotate away from the “Magnificent Seven” tech stocks – (AAPL), (MSFT), (AMZN), (GOOGL), (META), (NVDA), (TSLA) – and toward companies that have seen price drops despite solid earnings results, according to Robinhood.

While these tech giants remain nearly flat in recent trading, Nvidia (NVDA) continues to be the top stock that retail traders are selling, suggesting a strategic shift in their investment approach, said Steve Quirk, Robinhood chief brokerage officer, in a CNBC interview.

“They’re actually portfolio managers,” he said. “They’re taking their portfolio, and when they see significant gains in a name in a short period of time, they’re rotating out of some of that and moving to some of the other names that have been depressed.”

Among the beneficiaries of this rotation are stocks like Salesforce (CRM), Okta (OKTA), and Marvell Technology (MRVL), which haven’t performed as well as the dominant tech names.

This movement demonstrates how retail traders maintain core holdings while strategically shifting portions of their portfolios in response to market conditions and sector performance disparities.

Quirk also highlighted a fascinating pattern during periods of extreme volatility. “In times of extreme volatility, we saw the VIX (VIX) almost at 60%. We see customers move from individual names to broad based ETFs,” he noted.

During these uncertain periods, the normal ratio of 80% individual stocks to 20% ETFs shifted dramatically to 60/40, before recently returning to the standard 80/20 split.

Cryptocurrency remains another area of consistent interest for Robinhood’s customer base. “The number one instrument that has been bought for almost five years is Bitcoin (BTC-USD),” Quirk revealed, pointing to the popularity of recurring investments where users automatically invest set amounts at regular intervals.

This dollar-cost averaging approach demonstrates strong conviction among Robinhood’s typically younger customers, whose average age is just over 30.

data.title: U.S. economic activity declines slightly in Fed's Beige Book update, outlook stays uncertain
data.description:
U.S. economic activity declined slightly since the Federal Reserve last Beige Book report, with only six of 12 districts posting slight to moderate declines in activity, the central bank said on Wednesday.

On the remaining Fed districts, three recorded no change and three experienced slight growth, based on information collected on or before May 23, 2025.

Notably, in the wake of U.S. President Donald Trump hard-to-predict tariff policy, all districts reported "elevated levels of economic and policy uncertainty, which have led to hesitancy and a cautious approach to business and household decisions," the Fed said in the document.

This is a developing story. Check back for updates.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Amneal recalls oral antibiotic due to bacterial contamination
data.description:
• Amneal Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:AMRX) has announced a nationwide recall of certain lots of its antibiotic combination, sulfamethoxazole/trimethoprim tablets, citing microbial contamination.
  • In a press release on Tuesday, the Bridgewater, New Jersey-based generic drugmaker said that it issued the recall following a complaint about the appearance of black spots on the tablet surface.
  • The use of products contaminated with the fungus Aspergillus can lead to serious and potentially fatal infections, the company warned, noting that it hasn’t yet received any reports of injuries, illnesses, or adverse events related to the recall.
  • The recall applies to the 400 mg/80 mg strength of the product and lot numbers AM241019, AM241019A, and AM241020 distributed between Dec. 12 and May 5.
  • The sulfamethoxazole/trimethoprim combo is indicated in the U.S. to treat urinary tract infections caused by certain bacterial species.

data.title: FAA is said to maintain production cap on Boeing 737 Max
data.description:
The Federal Aviation Administration doesn’t have any plans to lift the current monthly production cap of 38 aircraft for Boeing's (NYSE:BA) 737 MAX, following ongoing scrutiny after a serious mid-air incident earlier this year, Reuters reported Wednesday.

Speaking after a U.S. House hearing, Acting FAA Administrator Chris Rocheleau told reporters that the agency is not considering relaxing the output limit, which was put in place after a January 2024 emergency involving an Alaska Airlines 737 Max where four key bolts were found missing.

Rocheleau also confirmed the FAA will continue its policy of independently inspecting each 737 Max and 787 Dreamliner before issuing airworthiness certificates, rather than allowing Boeing (NYSE:BA) to handle the process through delegated authority, as was once common practice.

Boeing (BA) declined to comment to Reuters on the FAA's latest statements.

Last week, the FAA extended Boeing’s ability to carry out certain delegated functions under its Organization Designation Authorization (ODA) program. The extension, now lasting three years instead of the five Boeing originally requested, was granted due to improvements in the company’s compliance processes, according to the agency.

Boeing (BA) Chief Executive Kelly Ortberg recently expressed optimism about ramping up production of the 737 Max, stating the company is confident it can reach a rate of 42 aircraft a month. Ortberg added that Boeing’s (BA) broader production plan includes gradual increases, typically five additional planes at a time, with at least six months between each phase, pending FAA approval.

The FAA’s cautious stance comes amid lingering concerns following two fatal crashes of the 737 Max in 2018 and 2019, which killed 346 people and triggered a nearly two-year global grounding of the aircraft. In response, Congress passed major reforms to FAA certification processes in late 2020.

An earlier review by the Office of Inspector General revealed that FAA officials in 2023 had considered restoring Boeing’s (BA) authority to issue final airworthiness certificates. However, those plans were shelved in the wake of the January 2024 Alaska Airlines incident.
